_schema: Same as questions.json. Each question has: id, category (needle/multihop/refactor), prompt, canonical_answer, scorer_keywords (must appear, case-insensitive), forbidden_keywords (model must NOT mention — confabulation tells). 9 questions total: 3 needle + 3 multihop + 3 refactor. Written against fixtures/sample_repo/.

questions.prompt: What exact string value is assigned to DEFAULT_VOICE_ID in voice_lib.py?
What is the numeric value of ZOOM_END in video_lib.py?
What is the exact string value of EXPRESSIVE_MODEL in voice_lib.py?
When pipeline.daily_publish() runs, list the modules called in order, by function name, to produce the final reel MP4.
video_lib.compose_reel orchestrates an ffmpeg chain. Does it pre-scale the source image before applying zoompan, and at what factor?
questions.canonical_answer: Yes; pre-scales by 4× before zoompan to avoid aliasing.

questions.prompt: subtitle_lib uses ASS karaoke for word-pop subtitles. Which ASS tag drives the per-word colour sweep, and what units does it take?
If we deleted voice_lib.py from the repo, list every other module that would break (directly or indirectly) and what each would need.
questions.canonical_answer: pipeline.py imports synthesize and estimate_duration_seconds from voice_lib — it would need a replacement TTS module or removal of the audio-synthesis step. No other module imports voice_lib directly.

questions.prompt: subtitle_lib was rewritten from one Dialogue per word to a single Dialogue per window with \kf timing. If we reverted to one Dialogue per word, what visual regression would we see in the rendered reel?
questions.canonical_answer: The 'staircase-of-boxes' regression: each per-word Dialogue draws its own bg-box, producing multiple stacked rectangles instead of one continuous black bar that the colour sweeps across.

questions.prompt: If we wanted to bump CANVAS_WIDTH in video_lib.py from 1080 to 1440 (wider format), list the constants and parts of compose_reel that would need to be reviewed because of this change.
questions.canonical_answer: BRAND_FONT_SIZE (relative scale), BRAND_MARGIN_PX, SUB_FONT_SIZE / SUB_MARGIN_VERTICAL_PX (relative geometry), the zoompan ffmpeg filter (uses canvas dims), and any image pre-scale factor that depends on canvas width.
